title: Today's AI Brief: Salesforce's AI-Powered Slackbot and More
summary: Salesforce's new Slackbot AI agent, a $2,000 AI-generated film debuts at Tribeca, and the evolving workplace AI landscape.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI
imageCaption: Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI
imageSourceTitle: Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI briefing. Big moves are happening in the world of workplace AI. Salesforce just rolled out a brand-new version of Slackbot, its workplace assistant. This isn't your old Slackbot – it's now a fully powered AI agent capable of handling complex tasks. I'm excited to see how this will change the way we interact with our work tools.

The old Slackbot was more of a notification tool. This new version uses advanced AI to understand and respond to workplace queries. It's a clear move by Salesforce to stay competitive with Microsoft and Google in the AI-powered workplace space. The question on my mind is – how will this change the way we interact with our work tools on a daily basis?

In a fascinating intersection of AI and art, a $2,000 AI-generated film called 'Dreams of Violets' is set to debut at Tribeca. This film tells the story of the Iranian government's mass killing of civilians. It's a powerful example of how AI can be used to create impactful content. I find it amazing to think that a film with such a significant message was created with a relatively modest budget.

Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Salesforce on Tuesday launched an entirely rebuilt version of Slackbot , the company's workplace assistant, transforming it from a simple notification tool into what executives describe as a fully powered AI agent capable of searching enterprise data, drafting documents, and taking action on behalf of employees. My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
